Document:
# Precautionary Charges - These are used to confine people indefinitely.
Charge
Description
Insanity
\- Declared by the [bold]Chief Medical Officer[/bold] or a synthetic, acting in such a manner which makes the offender not sound clear of mind.\nThe subject, once cleared to be of sound mind, may be released from this particular charge. \n\- In the absence of the above, the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] or [color=#8B0000][bold]Chief MP[/bold][/color] can appoint an [bold]Officer[/bold] of the medical department to perform an evaluation in their stead. If this is done, the appointed should receive an explanation on what an Insanity charge entails. \n\- In cases of verified suicide or attempted suicide, insanity may be applied without evaluation. The subject may appeal this, including to any medical personnel.
Prisoner of War
\- Being a hostile member of a legitimate and recognised nation, or any person recognised to be part of, or otherwise bearing the colors or insignia of the alleged "CLF" threat. \n\- Prisoners of war cannot be executed. See [textlink="Third-Parties" link="RMCMarineSOPNONModThirdparty"] for more information and exceptions.
Discretionary Detainment
\- A discretionary charge used by [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] to detain personnel for any reason, for the safety and benefit of the operation or security. \n\- This is only appealable to the [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color], or the [color=#ffa500][bold]Provost Marshal Office[/bold][/color], and may be lifted at any time by the [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color].
